recentQuizFindings: First answers are often too vague
Service troubleshooting still tends to skip endpoints
Component boundaries improve when phrased as exact roles
New scheduling/control topics are stronger when tied to exact component boundaries
Scheduler profiles remain conceptually weak and need repetition later
Application lifecycle topics are landing better than scheduler profile details
Backup and restore is stronger than expected once tied back to etcd state
Security access topics improved substantially after a mixed recap
Certificates still need sharper wording around public key vs private key
ConfigMaps landed well as a continuation of env vars and Secrets
Node selector logic is understandable after repair, but still not yet fully automatic

recommendedDrills: debug a Service with no endpoints
differentiate scheduler vs kubelet from symptom patterns
compare ClusterIP vs NodePort with exact troubleshooting chain
compare multiple schedulers vs scheduler profiles
contrast admission controllers with normal controllers
practice rolling updates and watch available replica behavior
compare multi-container Pods with init containers
compare manual scaling, HPA, and VPA
compare etcd snapshot restore with YAML-only restore
compare kubeconfig, RBAC, ServiceAccounts, and authentication flow
compare certificate, private key, and CA trust roles
compare ConfigMaps, env vars, and Secrets
compare nodeSelector with taints/tolerations and later affinity
